reasoning: Bayern Munich is 8 points clear of second-place Bayer Leverkusen with only 3 matches (9 possible points) remaining in the season, after both teams won their matches on April 26. To lose the title, Bayern would need to lose all three of their remaining matches (or earn at most 1 point, depending on the goal difference tiebreaker) while Bayer Leverkusen wins all three of their remaining matches. The compounded probability of these exact outcomes happening simultaneously is extremely low, making it practically certain but not yet mathematically certain that Bayern will win the 2024-25 Bundesliga. Given their dominant form and the small number of matches left, their victory is highly probable. I am capping my final estimate at 0.95 as per the guidelines' upper limit of bounded probabilities, to account for extreme black swan sports scenarios or a complete collapse.
